title: Capture Technician
description:
Job Summary: The Camera Technician will be primarily responsible for the end-to-end production of high-quality 360 virtual tours, encompassing scanning, processing, and client delivery. This role also involves executing professional photography and videography assignments using a range of equipment, alongside essential photo editing tasks. The ideal candidate is technically proficient, creatively driven, and committed to delivering outstanding visual content.


Key Responsibilities:


360 Virtual Tour Production:

  • Conduct on-site 360 virtual tour scans of properties and spaces using specialized 360 cameras/equipment (Real360 and Matterport).
  • Upload raw scan data to designated processing platforms.
  • Monitor the processing of virtual tour models, ensuring accuracy, quality and timely delivery.
  • Prepare and deliver final virtual tour products to clients timely via email.

Photography & Editing:

  • Capture high-quality interior and exterior photographs of properties and spaces using DSLR cameras and/or mobile device.
  • Perform comprehensive photo editing, including color correction, exposure adjustment, retouching, and cropping, to enhance image quality for all photographic and 360 tour visuals.
  • Ensure all photographic assets meet company standards for composition, lighting, and resolution.

Videography:

  • Shoot compelling video footage primarily using mobile devices for dynamic content.
  • Experience with more advanced videography equipment (e.g., mirrorless cameras, gimbals, drones) to capture professional-grade video content will be advantageous.
  • Apply fundamental videography principles including framing, lighting, and movement.

Equipment Management:

  • Maintain and care for all assigned camera, scanning, and lighting equipment.
  • Ensure all equipment is in good working order and ready for assignments.

Requirements & Qualifications:

  • Proven experience (minimum 2 years preferred) in 360 virtual tour scanning and/or photography/videography.
  • Proven photography and videography skills using mobile devices, with strong experience or knowledge of more advanced videography equipment and techniques (e.g., using lighting, gimbals).
  • Proficiency in photo editing software (e.g., Adobe Photoshop, Lightroom).
  • Familiarity with 360 virtual tour platforms (e.g., Matterport) is highly advantageous.
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects efficiently.
  • Strong attention to detail and a keen eye for visual aesthetics.
  • Excellent time management and organizational skills.
  • Possession of own professional camera equipment (DSLR) is a plus.

Good to Have:

  • Well-Presented: Professional demeanour and appearance when interacting with clients on-site.
  • Responsible: Highly reliable, punctual, and committed to meeting deadlines and ensuring deliverable quality.
  • Team Player: Able to collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients to achieve goals.
